contents.text: The brewing stand will not be needed here. Ars Elixirum introduces the [color=#EC8FFF]Glass Cauldron[/color] - a transparent vessel designed for alchemical work. Place it somewhere convenient, you will return to it often. The cauldron requires [color=#EC8FFF]heat from below[/color]. A campfire, magma block, or similar source will work. Without heat, the process will not begin.

Use a [color=#EC8FFF]Water Bucket[/color] on the cauldron. The water will begin to heat. Wait until it [color=#EC8FFF]reaches a boil[/color] before adding anything. Bubbles and sound will let you know when it is ready.

Now the mixture takes shape. Most organic items carry alchemical properties - herbs, food, familiar potion ingredients, and more.

The cauldron accepts up to [color=#EC8FFF]three ingredients[/color]. Each contributes its own essences, while also taking on a specific role:

rows: Provides essences like any ingredient. Also defines the [color=#EC8FFF]Focus[/color] of the elixir, how its effects lean between potency and duration.
Provides essences and shapes the mixture. Determines the [color=#EC8FFF]Form[/color] of the elixir, drinkable, splash, or lingering.
Provides essences and influences stability. Defines the [color=#EC8FFF]Risk[/color], affecting both the chance of side effects and how stable the brew is during creation.

contents.text: Order matters. The first ingredient becomes the [color=#8fe1ff]Base[/color], the second the [color=#8fe1ff]Catalyst[/color], and the third the [color=#8fe1ff]Inhibitor[/color].

Once all three ingredients are added, use a [color=#EC8FFF]Glass Bottle[/color] on the cauldron. One portion is drawn as an elixir. Each fill holds [color=#EC8FFF]three portions[/color], allowing you to bottle the same mixture multiple times. The result may not be perfect, but it is a beginning.
